worker_task: Create a new alpha candidate file in alphaforge/src/alphaforge/candidates/ named momentum_alpha.py that implements a simple 20-day momentum factor (close(0)/close(20) - 1). The file should contain a class `MomentumAlpha` with a `generate_signals(data)` method that takes a DataFrame with a 'close' column and returns a Series of signals. Also create a companion test script in the same directory called test_momentum_alpha.py that loads sample price data (use synthetic data or a small CSV), runs the alpha, and prints the IC (information coefficient) and Sharpe ratio of the signals over a training period (train_start='2020-01-01', train_end='2022-12-31') and a test period (test_start='2023-01-01', test_end='2023-12-31'). The test script must output the required report fields: train_start, train_end, test_start, test_end, n_combinations (set to 1 since this is a single parameter), and metric (report the IC value for the test period). Ensure both files are created under alphaforge/src/alphaforge/candidates/ only. After running the test, include the completion summary with the required fields.
rationale: This is a small, safe, reversible first iteration. It creates a simple deterministic momentum alpha and a test script that validates it, producing the required gate report fields. No existing files are modified, and all new files go into the sandbox path.
success_criteria: MomentumAlpha class defined with generate_signals method
Test script outputs train_start, train_end, test_start, test_end, n_combinations, metric
risk_notes: Low risk. No existing files modified. Ensure the test script uses synthetic data to avoid external dependencies. The gate will fail if any file is written outside the sandbox path.
